steps.title: Use cubic regression to avoid sign errors
steps.content:
Because the highest power is $x^3$, a quick Desmos check is to fit the original expression to $ax^3+bx^2+cx+d$:
$$x_1=[-2,-1,0,1]$$
$$y_1=(6x_1^3-6x_1+4)-(7x_1^2+7x_1-2)$$
$$y_1\sim ax_1^3+bx_1^2+cx_1+d$$

steps.title: Read the expression
steps.content:
The regression gives $a=6$, $b=-7$, $c=-13$, and $d=6$. So the equivalent expression is:
$$6x^3-7x^2-13x+6$$

steps.title: Confirm by distributing
steps.content:
The minus sign changes every sign in the second parentheses:
$$(6x^3-6x+4)-(7x^2+7x-2)=6x^3-6x+4-7x^2-7x+2$$
Then combine the $x$ terms and constants:
$$6x^3-7x^2-13x+6$$

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice C is the same expression, just ordered as $-7x^2+6x^3-13x+6$. The other choices either add exponents into $x^9$ or use the wrong linear/constant terms. Therefore C is correct.
